exchange.title: Exchange
exchange.subtitle: Simulated CEX trading with fake USD
exchange.helpMarketOrderDesc: A market order executes immediately at the current best available price. You get your crypto right away, but the exact price may differ slightly from what's shown (called 'slippage').
exchange.helpFeeDesc: Exchanges charge a small percentage fee on each trade. Typically 0.1% to 0.5%. This is how exchanges make money.
exchange.helpOrderBookDesc: The order book shows all pending buy orders (bids, in green) and sell orders (asks, in red). The gap between the highest bid and lowest ask is called the 'spread'.

wallet.title: My Wallet
wallet.subtitle: Manage your simulated keys, addresses, and balances
wallet.createDesc: Generate a simulated wallet with a seed phrase, public address, and private key for research purposes.
wallet.seedWarning: In real crypto, NEVER share your seed phrase. Anyone with these words can steal all your funds. This is a simulation.
wallet.privKeyWarning: Never share your private key. This is for educational display only.
wallet.helpSeedDesc: A seed phrase (or mnemonic) is a set of 12-24 words that serves as the master backup for a crypto wallet. From these words, all your private keys and addresses can be regenerated.
wallet.helpAddressDesc: Your public address is like a bank account number - it's safe to share. Others use it to send you crypto. It's derived from your public key using a hash function.
wallet.helpPrivKeyDesc: A private key is a secret number that proves ownership and allows you to sign transactions. It's like the password to your bank account - never share it.

txFactory.title: Transaction Factory
txFactory.subtitle: Craft transactions and observe gas mechanics in real-time
txFactory.noTx: No transactions yet. Send one to see it in action.
txFactory.helpGasDesc: Gas is the fee you pay to get your transaction processed by the network. Higher gas = faster confirmation. Think of it like tipping a delivery driver - more tip means faster delivery.
txFactory.helpGweiDesc: Gwei is a tiny unit of Ethereum (0.000000001 ETH). Gas prices are measured in Gwei. When the network is busy, Gwei prices go up because everyone is competing to get their transactions processed.
txFactory.helpGasLimitDesc: Gas Limit is the maximum amount of computation your transaction can use. A simple transfer uses 21,000 gas units. Smart contract interactions use more. You pay: Gas Price x Gas Used.
txFactory.helpMempoolDesc: The mempool (memory pool) is a waiting area for unconfirmed transactions. When you send a transaction, it goes to the mempool first. Miners/validators pick transactions with higher fees first.
txFactory.helpNonceDesc: A nonce is a sequential counter for each transaction from your address. It prevents replay attacks and ensures transactions are processed in order. Nonce 0 = your first transaction, Nonce 1 = second, etc.
txFactory.explainIdle: Submit a transaction to see it flow through the network.
txFactory.explainPending: Your transaction ({{gwei}} Gwei) entered the mempool. Miners prioritize higher-fee transactions.
txFactory.explainStuck: Low gas fee — your transaction is stuck waiting. Other higher-fee transactions jump ahead.
txFactory.explainConfirmed: Your transaction was picked by a miner and included in the next block. Confirmed!

explorer.title: Block Explorer
explorer.subtitle: Visualize the chain and test immutability by tampering with block data
explorer.chainBrokenDesc: Tampering was detected — subsequent block hashes no longer match.
explorer.chainValidDesc: All blocks are properly linked. The chain is intact.
explorer.helpPrevHashDesc: Each block stores the hash of the previous block, forming a chain. If any block is tampered with, its hash changes and breaks the link to the next block.
explorer.tamperDesc: Editing block data will change its hash, breaking the chain from this point forward.

mining.title: Mining / Validators
mining.subtitle: Experience Proof-of-Work mining and Proof-of-Stake validation
mining.helpDifficultyDesc: Difficulty determines how many leading zeros the block hash must have. More zeros = harder to find a valid hash = more computation needed.
mining.foundDesc: Found a valid hash after {{attempts}} attempts in {{time}}s
mining.helpNonceDesc: In mining, the nonce is the number that miners change repeatedly to find a hash that meets the difficulty target. It's the trial-and-error variable in the mining process.
mining.posDesc: In PoS, validators are selected based on the amount of crypto they stake (lock up) as collateral.

nft.title: NFT & Token Studio
nft.subtitle: Create tokens and watch the smart contract execute line-by-line
nft.helpSymbolDesc: A token symbol is a short ticker (like BTC, ETH) that identifies your token on exchanges and wallets. Usually 3-5 uppercase letters.
nft.helpContractDesc: A smart contract is self-executing code stored on the blockchain. When you mint an NFT, the contract code runs automatically to create and assign ownership of the token.

forensics.title: Forensics & Privacy
forensics.subtitle: Investigate transaction graphs and understand mixing/tumbling techniques
forensics.helpMixerDesc: A crypto mixer (or tumbler) pools funds from many users, shuffles them, and sends different coins back. This breaks the on-chain link between sender and receiver.
forensics.clickTrace: Click 'Trace Next' to follow the money trail.
forensics.mixerIdle: Press Start Mix to visualize the tumbling process.
forensics.phaseDone: Mix complete — original trail is broken.
forensics.mixerExplain: The mixer breaks the direct link between your input and output addresses by pooling funds with other users and redistributing different coins.

portal.title: Crypto Portal
portal.subtitle: Curated resources for blockchain research and analysis
portal.explorersDesc: Inspect on-chain transactions and wallet activity
portal.dataDesc: Real-time market data, DeFi analytics, and on-chain metrics
portal.newsDesc: Stay informed with the latest crypto industry developments
portal.toolsDesc: Essential tools for wallet safety and network configuration

mobileWallet.title: Mobile Wallet Demo
mobileWallet.subtitle: Experience a mobile crypto wallet — tap through screens like a real app
mobileWallet.instructions: Interact with the phone mockup to explore wallet features
mobileWallet.bannerDesc: Trade, earn, and manage crypto all in one place
mobileWallet.createWalletDesc: Create a new wallet or import an existing one to get started
mobileWallet.seedPhraseWarning: Write these words down and keep them safe. Never share them with anyone.
mobileWallet.emailVerifyDesc: Enter your email to receive a verification code
mobileWallet.qrLabel: Scan QR code to send funds
mobileWallet.buyDesc: Purchase crypto instantly with simulated USD
mobileWallet.sellDesc: Convert crypto back to simulated USD
mobileWallet.swapDesc: Exchange one crypto for another instantly

banner.warning: Simulation Environment - For Educational Research Only. No Real Funds Involved.
